perturbation.original_text: (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes].
perturbation.changed_text: (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The clause 'provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes' has been omitted. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because, without this clause, it appears that MD Anderson relinquishes all rights to the Inventions, conflicting with Section 6.4, which outlines MD Anderson's right to publish Research data and information and Section 7.3, which grants LBIO license to MD Anderson's Background Intellectual Property. Omitting MD Anderson's right to use the invention affects the interpretation of rights related to publications and data usage throughout the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MD Anderson agrees to provide LBIO with a copy of any manuscript, abstract or other proposed publication or presentation relating to the Research or the Materials (a "Publication"), prior to submission thereof to a publisher or to any third party, and in any case, not less than 45 days prior to any public disclosure, for the purpose of protecting proprietary or intellectual property of LBIO that might be contained in such Publication. Following receipt of such proposed Publication, LBIO shall have the right to cause MD Anderson to (i) withhold publication or other public disclosure thereof for a period of up to 90 days in order to provide LBIO time to obtain appropriate intellectual property protection thereof, and (ii) remove any proprietary, or otherwise confidential, information of LBIO contained in such Publication (excluding Research results). In any event, MD Anderson will not disclose proprietary, or otherwise confidential, information in an "unblinded" manner when it can be done so in a "blinded" manner. In the event of any Publication (including any public presentation relating to the Research or the Materials), MD Anderson agrees to acknowledge LBIO and/or give credit to LBIO scientists, as scientifically appropriate, based on any contribution they may have made to the work which shall be in accordance with any relevant policies and guidelines of the publication, presentation forum, as well as policies and guidelines of general applicability, such as the International Committee of Medical Journal Editors recommendations. In addition, to the extent that it is legally able to do so, MD Anderson hereby grants LBIO a royalty-free right and license to use and reproduce any Publication. LBIO shall be acknowledged as a financial collaborator of the Study reported in a Publication.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows MD Anderson to use the invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes. The changed text removes this right, creating a situation where MD Anderson's ability to publish research data (as described in the Contradicted Text) is now in conflict with the changed text, which appears to assign all rights to LBIO. The removal of the clause creates a clear contradiction because MD Anderson no longer possesses an explicit right to use the Inventions for the purposes described in the Contradicted Text, like publishing research data related to the Inventions. This affects MD Anderson's ability to fulfill its obligations and LBIO's rights regarding intellectual property, leading to inconsistency within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: LBIO shall be permitted to publicly disclose the existence of this Agreement, and the title and purpose of each clinical Study, in LBIO's electronic materials, printed materials, oral presentations, and press releases, and LBIO shall be permitted to include each clinical Study as a component of LBIO's clinical product pipeline.
perturbation.changed_text: LBIO shall be permitted to publicly disclose the existence of this Agreement in LBIO's electronic materials, printed materials, oral presentations, and press releases.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'and the title and purpose of each clinical Study, in LBIO's electronic materials, printed materials, oral presentations, and press releases, and LBIO shall be permitted to include each clinical Study as a component of LBIO's clinical product pipeline' an in-text contradiction is created. This phrase, is removed LBIO's ability to disclose specific details about the clinical study. However, Section 6.4 references 'any public presentation relating to the Research or the Materials', indicating that some level of disclosure about the research is anticipated and agreed upon, which creates the contradiction. With the omission, it is unclear what level of detail LBIO can disclose about the clinical studies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MD Anderson agrees to provide LBIO with a copy of any manuscript, abstract or other proposed publication or presentation relating to the Research or the Materials (a "Publication"), prior to submission thereof to a publisher or to any third party, and in any case, not less than 45 days prior to any public disclosure, for the purpose of protecting proprietary or intellectual property of LBIO that might be contained in such Publication. Following receipt of such proposed Publication, LBIO shall have the right to cause MD Anderson to (i) withhold publication or other public disclosure thereof for a period of up to 90 days in order to provide LBIO time to obtain appropriate intellectual property protection thereof, and (ii) remove any proprietary, or otherwise confidential, information of LBIO contained in such Publication (excluding Research results). In any event, MD Anderson will not disclose proprietary, or otherwise confidential, information in an "unblinded" manner when it can be done so in a "blinded" manner. In the event of any Publication (including any public presentation relating to the Research or the Materials), MD Anderson agrees to acknowledge LBIO and/or give credit to LBIO scientists, as scientifically appropriate, based on any contribution they may have made to the work which shall be in accordance with any relevant policies and guidelines of the publication, presentation forum, as well as policies and guidelines of general applicability, such as the International Committee of Medical Journal Editors recommendations. In addition, to the extent that it is legally able to do so, MD Anderson hereby grants LBIO a royalty-free right and license to use and reproduce any Publication. LBIO shall be acknowledged as a financial collaborator of the Study reported in a Publication.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed LBIO to disclose the existence, title, and purpose of each clinical study. The changed text omits the permission to disclose the title and purpose of each clinical study, creating a situation where LBIO's ability to disclose specific details is restricted. However, Section 6.4, the contradicted text, anticipates 'any public presentation relating to the Research or the Materials,' implying that some level of disclosure is expected and agreed upon. This creates a contradiction because the changed text limits disclosure, while the contradicted text anticipates it, making it unclear what level of detail LBIO can disclose about the clinical studies. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will establish a joint steering committee ("JSC") of equal representation, comprised of three members from each Party, with the members of each Party collectively having one vote on all matters to be decided upon by the JSC. Each Party can appoint and replace its members in the JSC at its own discretion through timely written notice to the other Party. The Principal Investigators for each Study (as defined hereinafter) shall attend each JSC meeting, except in the event of exigent circumstances that do not permit such attendance.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will establish a joint steering committee ("JSC") of equal representation, comprised of three members from each Party, with the members of each Party collectively having one vote on all matters to be decided upon by the JSC. Each Party can appoint and replace its members in the JSC at its own discretion through timely written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the sentence 'The Principal Investigators for each Study (as defined hereinafter) shall attend each JSC meeting, except in the event of exigent circumstances that do not permit such attendance' creates an in-text contradiction. While the amended clause establishes the structure and member replacement procedure of the JSC, other parts of the agreement, particularly Section 2.2(a), refer to the responsibilities and importance of the Principal Investigators in the study. Without their mandatory attendance at JSC meetings, as formerly stated, it creates uncertainty regarding how their input is incorporated into decision-making, thus creating a contradiction regarding the study's oversight and coordination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Principal Investigator. Each Study Order will identify the individual that will serve as the "Principal Investigator" for the relevant Study at MD Anderson and shall be responsible for MD Anderson's administration and supervision of its portion of such Study. If the originally named Principal Investigator becomes unable or unwilling to continue a Study for any reason, MD Anderson shall propose a substitute Principal Investigator with comparable qualifications within two business days of MD Anderson becoming aware of such event. If the proposed candidate is not available or is not acceptable to LBIO, LBIO may terminate the applicable Study in accordance with Section 8.3(ii).
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the Principal Investigators attend JSC meetings, which is directly relevant to their responsibilities outlined in the contradicted text, Section 2.2(a). By removing this mandatory attendance, the changed text creates a contradiction. Section 2.2(a) identifies the Principal Investigator as responsible for MD Anderson's administration and supervision of the study. Removing their mandatory attendance at JSC meetings, where key decisions about the study are made, creates uncertainty regarding how their input is incorporated into decision-making, thus creating a contradiction regarding the study's oversight and coordination. This creates a direct conflict between the changed text and the contradicted text because it weakens the role and responsibilities of the principal investigator as defined in the contradicted text.
